distribution_plot.caption: Distribution of 2 categories across 5,630 customers. Pie chart chosen for readability at this category count.

stacked_churn_plot.caption: Each bar spans 100% of customers inside that category. Red = churn rate. Orange dashed line = overall retention baseline — any category whose red segment extends PAST this line churns more than average (a hotspot).

chi_squared.narration: χ² = 4.66 on 1 df. Marginally significant (p = 0.0308). Tests whether the category proportions differ between churn and retained.

cramers_v.narration: Cramér's V = 0.029 — weak association with churn.

narrative.distribution: 'gender' has 2 category/ies across 5,630 customers. Most common: 'Male' (60.1%). Least common: 'Female' (39.9%).
narrative.churn_rates: Overall churn rate = 16.8%. Highest-churn category: 'Male' at 17.7% (1.05× the baseline). Lowest-churn category: 'Female' at 15.5% (0.92× the baseline).
narrative.association: χ² = 4.66 on 1 df. Marginally significant (p = 0.0308). Tests whether the category proportions differ between churn and retained. Cramér's V = 0.029 — weak association with churn.

recommendation.rationale: Weak association (Cramér's V = 0.029). One-hot encode for completeness but deprioritise in feature selection.
